uk: Неділя 23-тя по Зісланні Святого Духа. Глас 6. Єв. 1.
🕃 Свв. чудотворців і безсрібників Косми й Дам'яна.
saints.en: 22nd Sunday after Pentecost. Hieromartyr Theodore Romzha, Bishop of Mukachevo. Sts. Unmercenaries and Wonderworkers Cosmas & Damian

rubrics:
Вечірня: «Блажен муж». На «Господи, взиваю» - 10 стихир: 4 - воскресних, 6 - свщмч., Слава: свщмч., І нині: богородичний-догмат 6-го гласу. Вхід. Прокімен дня і три читання свщмч.: Прип. 10:6-8, 8:6-21; Прип. 10:31–11:12; Муд. 4:7-15. На Литії: перша стихира – храму, також стихири свщмч., Слава: свщмч., І нині: богородичний з недільної стиховні за гласом наславника. Стиховня воскресна, Слава: свщмч., І нині: богородичний з недільної стиховні за гласом «Слава». На благословенні хлібів: «Богородице Діво» (2) і тропар свщмч. (1). Відпуст великий недільний з помин. свщмч.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар воскресний (2), Слава: свщмч., І нині: богородичний з недільних за гласом тропаря свщмч. Катизми чергові і сідальні воскресні, також Полієлей з величаннями свщмч. «Ангельський собор», іпакой 6-го гласу, також 1-й і 2-й сідальний свщмч., Слава: 3-й сідальний свщмч., І нині: його богородичний. Степенна і прокімен 5-го гласу. Єв. – Мт. 116 зач.; 28, 16-20. Все інше до канону – неділі. Канон воскресний, богород. і свщмч. Катавасія: «Відкрию уста мої». По 3-ій пісні – кондак-ікос і сідальний ап. По 6-ій – кондак-ікос неділі. По 9-ій – світильний недільний, Сл.: свщмч., І нині: богород недільного світильного. Хвалитних стихир – 8: 4 воскресних і 4 свщмч. (дві останні – з приспівами свщмч.), Слава: стихира євангельська, І нині: «Преблагословенна». По великому славослов'ї – тропар «Воскресши з гробу». Відпуст великий недільний з поминанням свщмч.
Часи: на всіх часах: тропар воскресний, також Слава: свщмч., кондаки: на 1-му і 6-му – недільний, на 3-му і 9-му – свщмч.
Літургія: антифони: два перших – воскресні, а третій – звичайний з воскресним приспівом, а також і «Прийдіте, поклонімся». Тропар воскресний і свщмч., кондак воскресний, Слава: свщмч., І нині: богородичний за гласом наславника. Прокімен, апостол, алилуя, євангелія і причасний – спочатку неділі, а після – свщмч. Ряд.: Ап. – Еф. 220 зач.; 2, 4-10. Єв. – Лк. 38 зач.; 8, 26-39. Свщнмч: Ап. – Євр. 334 зач.; 13, 7-16. Єв. – Лк. зач. 67.; 12, 32-40.

troparion_en.text: Holy unmercenaries and wonderworkers, Cosmas and Damian, / heal our infirmities. / Freely you have received; freely you give to us.

kontakion_en.text: Having received the grace of healing, / you grant healing to those in need. / Glorious wonder workers and healers, Cosmas and Damian, / visit us and put down the insolence of our enemies, / and bring healing to the world through your miracles.

matched_oca_saint: Holy Wonderworkers and Unmercenaries Cosmas and Damian of Mesopotamia

oca_saints.name: Holy Wonderworkers and Unmercenaries Cosmas and Damian of Mesopotamia

troparion.text: Holy unmercenaries and wonderworkers, Cosmas and Damian, / heal our infirmities. / Freely you have received; freely you give to us.

kontakion.text: Having received the grace of healing, / you grant healing to those in need. / Glorious wonder workers and healers, Cosmas and Damian, / visit us and put down the insolence of our enemies, / and bring healing to the world through your miracles.

oca_saints.name: Hieromartyrs John the Bishop and Jacob (James) the Presbyter, of Persia

troparion.text: O God of our Fathers,  /  always act with kindness towards us;  / take not Your mercy from us,  /  but guide our lives in peace  /  through the prayers our holy fathers John and Jacob.

kontakion.text: (Podoben: “As the first fruits...”) The Church glorifies you as teachers of virtue and adornments of hierarchs,  / singing: “By your intercessions for those who lovingly honor you, /  grant progress in virtue, and deliverance from temptations.”

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Kyriaina and Juliana in Cilicia

troparion.text: As reasonable lambs, you were guided by Christ, the Chief Shepherd, / along the path of martyrdom. / You finished your course and kept the faith; / therefore, honored Kyriaina and Juliana, / with joyful hearts, we magnify Christ today, as we honor your holy memory!

oca_saints.name: Martyr Hermenegild the Goth of Spain

troparion.text:
Your Martyr Hermenegild, O Lord,  /  through his suffering received an incorruptible crown from You, our 
God;  /  for possessed of Your strength, he laid low the tyrants  / and shattered the powerless boldness of 
the demons.  /  By his supplications, save our souls.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You shone forth in your splendor as a bright star, announcing Christ to this temporal world, /  O Martyr
Hermenegild vanquishing the snares of false gods,  / you revealed the true light to the faithful,  / and you 
always intercede on behalf of us all.

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Cæsarius, Dacius, Savva, Sabinian, Agrippa, Adrian, and Thomas, at Damascus

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Monastic Martyrs James and his two disciples, James the Deacon, and Dionysios the Monk

troparion.text:
(Podoben: “Thou Who wast lifted up...”)
Living angelically on earth, you received the grace of the Apostles,  / and you were sent forth as a herald and teacher of repentance,  / indicating the path of salvation to those who wished.  / Therefore, you arrayed yourself nobly for the contest, O wise one,  / along with your steadfast fellow-athletes, O greatest Monastic Martyr James.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “O Victorious Leader...”)
Let us praise James, as is fitting, /  the illustrious and divine trumpet of repentance, and holy preacher of God's love for mankind;  / for as he bore the wounds of the Lord upon himself,  / he fulfilled what was lacking in the martyrs.  / Therefore, we say: Rejoice, most excellent Father James.

troparion.text:
(Podoben: “Come, let us worship the Word...”)
Let us honor the most fragrant flower of purity,  / the boast and divine offspring of Sinope, /  the Virgin Martyr of Christ,  / the most pure Helen who struggled steadfastly,  / and overcame the Enemy by the power of faith, /  and she intercedes fervently with Christ God for everyone,  / that He may have mercy on our souls.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You appeared today...”)
As an undefiled virgin,  / you overcame the most cunning Enemy by the power of Christ,  / O all-praiseworthy Virgin Martyr, Helen.
